concepts.title: Condition for tangency and points of contact
concepts.conceptBody:
Tangent to the Hyperbola

Condition for tangency and points of contact : The condition for the line $y=mx+c$ to be a tangent to the hyperbola $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}-\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$ is that ${{c}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}}$ and the

coordinates of the points of contact are $\left( \pm \frac{{{a}^{2}}m}{\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}}}},\pm \frac{{{b}^{2}}}{\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}}}} \right)$

concepts.title: Equation of tangent
concepts.conceptBody:
Equation of tangent 

a) Point form: The equation of the tangent to the hyperbola $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}-\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$ at the point $\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$ is $ $

The equation of tangent at $\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}_{1}} \right)$ can also be obtained by replacing ${{\text{x}}^{2}}$ by $\text{x}{{\text{x}}_{1}},{{\text{y}}^{2}}\text{y}{{\text{y}}_{1}},\text{x}$ by $\frac{\text{x}+{{\text{x}}_{1}}}{2}\text{y}$ by $\frac{\text{y}+{{\text{y}}_{1}}}{2}$ and $\text{xy}$ by $\frac{\text{x}{{\text{y}}_{1}}+{{\text{x}}_{1}}\text{y}}{2}$. This method is used only when the equation of hyperbola is a polynomial of second degree in $\text{x}$ and $\text{y}$.

(b) Parametric Form : The eqn. of the tangent to the hyperbola $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}-\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$ at the point $\left( \text{asec}\theta ,\text{b}\text{tan}\theta \right)$ is $ $

(c) Slope Form : The equation of tangent to the hyperbola $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}-\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=-1$ in terms of slope ' $m$ ' is $y=mx\pm \sqrt{{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}}}$ The coordinates of the points of contact are

$\left( \pm \frac{{{a}^{2}}m}{\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}}}},\pm \frac{{{b}^{2}}}{\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}}}} \right)$

concepts.title: Number of Tangents From a Point
concepts.conceptBody:
Number of tangents from a point

Two tangents can be drawn from a point to a hyperbola. The two tangents are real and distinct or coincident of imaginary according as the given point lies outside, on or inside the hyperbola.
